title: For Those with No Children
scripture_text: He gives the barren woman a home, making her the joyous mother of children. Praise the Lord.
reflection: Your hands are not empty. They have held other things. The Lord is not punishing you. He is shaping you.
body:
The question you cannot stop being asked. The announcement on social media. The baby shower you RSVP'd no to.

Your arms are not empty. They have held other things. Books, friends, second-graders, your partner, a sick neighbor, a Sunday school class, a thousand small kindnesses that no one will ever put on a card.

The Lord sees your longing. He honors it. He is not punishing you. He is not withholding. He is shaping you into a person with a particular kind of strength — the strength of those who have learned to hold loosely, to love past the absence, to find the family in the friends, to make a home out of a life that did not look the way they planned.

You are not less. You are not behind. You are beloved.
prayer: Lord, hold my longing. Show me the children I have. Make me a mother in ways I did not plan. Amen.
